calendar.matters:
Petition For Modification Of Trust Terms
Off Calendar Jul-28-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Byron Done appeared by zoom for Petitioner.

The Court stated that it doesn't quite understand what happened here, questions that if Ms. Baird and Bruno exercised their right of first refusal, then why did any of the trust asset get transferred, why this Court should be used as a tool to reduce economic costs to the two Beneficiaries, why this Court should approve this petition when the property transferred properly to their Siblings and then to themselves, noted that they took it upon themselves to change the terms of the trust prior to filing this petition and questions why this wasn't done ten years ago. Mr. Done stated that two of Matilda's Children passed away during administration of this trust, that they need to get this ruling in order to transfer George's share and that the other Children did not want to purchase the property. Discussion was held with regard to whether or not the right of first refusal was exercised. Mr. Done stated that Mary and Bruno decided to trade properties, that there is an appeal at the Recorder's Office regarding assessment of 80% of the property, that it is not fair for them to not get the parent child exclusion, that they did purchase 20% from George's estate and inherited some from the trust as well as purchased some from the trust. The Court stated that the Settlor was clear as to what she wanted done with the property and questions how can it not be a material purpose? Mr. Done stated that given the change in circumstances with the Beneficiaries it's not a change in terms and that it is an exchange of their rights. The Court noted that it does not have the consent of all of the Beneficiaries. Mr. Done stated that he has consents of the vested Beneficiaries, but not the contingent Beneficiaries and that they are no longer Beneficiaries at his point. The Court noted that the parties want to modify the trust to make 100% of the property qualify for the parent to child transfer and stated that it will not do that. Mr. Done agrees that paragraphs four and five of the proposed order are not essential. The Court suggested taking this petition off calendar and Mr. Done filing a new petition under Probate Code 15409 largely based on these facts, but particularly on changed circumstances. Mr. Done withdraws his petition. The Petition for Modification of Trust Terms is off calendar. Mr. Done's new petition is to be given a hearing that falls on a Tuesday so that it can go before Judge Quinn.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
